title: Before baseline for manual grep based crypto inventory
claim: Manual grep and command-line inspection requires several days to roughly two work weeks for the current demo scope.
claim_ko: 현재 데모 범위를 사람이 수동 grep과 명령행 도구로 점검하면 수일에서 약 2주 수준의 작업이 필요하다.

assumptions.operator_profile: security engineer with shell access and OpenSSL/SSH tooling experience

steps.label: Scope and shell access preparation
steps.basis: Collect target list, confirm access path, prepare grep/find/openssl/ssh-keygen commands.

steps.label: Manual protocol checks
steps.basis: 33 discovered endpoints x 15-30 minutes for openssl s_client, ssh-keyscan, STARTTLS/IKE-specific checks, and notes.

steps.label: Host file and configuration grep
steps.basis: 10 host-agent candidate systems x 45-90 minutes for recursive certificate/key/config searches.

steps.label: Parse and deduplicate crypto assets
steps.basis: 67 expected crypto assets x 6-15 minutes for parsing, fingerprinting, family classification, and duplicate checks.

steps.label: CBOM recording, review, and handoff
steps.basis: Manual spreadsheet/CBOM entry, peer review, and evidence packaging.

presentation_guidance: Use this as a qualitative baseline, not as a measured benchmark.
Phrase the claim as 'manual inventory can easily take days to weeks depending on access and review friction'.
Contrast it with automated demo/runtime measurements only after the system-side duration is measured.
